title: SHOCKED: No shortcut to create task?
excerpt:
Loving Monday.com - but can not believe this short cut doesn’t exist. 
It is time - even if you have to be using the desktop app instead of the web app - we need a shortcut to create a new task (or row).

posts.author: mevangelidis (Michael E)
posts.cookedHtml:
Loving Monday.com - but can not believe this short cut doesn’t exist.

It is time - even if you have to be using the desktop app instead of the web app - we need a shortcut to create a new task (or row).

posts.author: jacobatmodus (Jacob)
posts.cookedHtml:
There are shortcuts for creating new Groups and new Columns, but not one for creating new tasks directly from the keyboard.

Would love a “Shift+Enter” or something of the sort to create a new task without having to click through it. I understand that selecting which Group to create the task on could be an issue, but it would be fairly intuitive to create them on the last Group interacted with, or give you the option (via “Shift+Down” etc) to choose which group it gets entered into.

The use-cases here are infinite, it just makes creating new tasks that much easier.

posts.author: EMorton (Erik Morton)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree… especially since the ability to create a new item in a group is present (using Shift +Enter of course) but only while already actively adding items (i.e. while editing the title of one task/item, using the shortcut to create a new one).

posts.author: sanjayananda (Sanjayananda)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, hope you’re all well.

I’d like to be able to create a new item in a table from the keyboard, without needing to click with a mouse.

Intuitively, a shift-return or cmd-return hotkey would make sense to me.

Hope this is helpful.

posts.author: cfraticelli (Celene Fraticelli)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree! This is something that ClickUp has that I absolutely LOVE!

There are so many hotkeys that let you skip clicking all together. This helps out those of us who aren’t keen on using the mouse, but also lets those who enjoy using a mouse to do their thing. This is a improvement that I think a lot of people will appreciate.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed.

We’ve got shortcuts for Create New Group (Ctrl+Shift+G) and Create New Column (Ctrl+Shift+C).

We could definitely use a Create New Item shortcut. Hopefully it already exists and we haven’t found it yet.

posts.author: cfraticelli (Celene Fraticelli)
posts.cookedHtml: Wow! I didn’t know that. Thanks for the tip!

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
All Monday.com shortcuts are listed here:
https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005339905-The-Spreadsheet-capabilities-shortcuts-?abcb=74582

posts.author: cfraticelli (Celene Fraticelli)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the link!

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml: In fact, if you click on your profile avatar in the bottom LHS sidebar, you’ll find a link to the shortcuts in the Explore section:

posts.author: JerryD (Jerry David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Anything progressed with this feature request? I am not able to find anything new.
Many missing keyboard shortcuts - such as delete line, copy, paste, new line, and more.

posts.author: Simonga25 (George Simon)
posts.cookedHtml: Being able to create a new item from the keyboard would by far be the most valuable keyboard shortcut.

posts.author: patowens86 (Patrick)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree that this would be one of the most useful shortcuts in Monday. What’s the status on this?

posts.author: pbriggs28 (Leo Briggs)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is absolute nonsense… If I could only have two shortcuts they would be quick search and quick add…

When you implement this, please allow adding the task to a specific workspace/board/section/etc. and adding labels/assignee/date/etc. all from the quick add bar. Look at how ToDoist implemented this as an example.

I was thinking about switching to monday.com for Personal and my business but quick navigation/entry is a must-have for me…

posts.author: Smittyto15 (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml: Any work on shortcut for adding of Item ?

posts.author: PM-CBC (Paolo)
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely needed. We will move to an alternative if it won’t be implemented within a week.

posts.author: jacobatmodus (Jacob)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this? Seems like a pretty basic feature to be missing.

posts.author: RuyAguilar (ruy.aguilar)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree. Pretty basic and pretty ridiculous that 2 years later this still is not implemented.

posts.author: Kori.Hare (Kori Hare)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a Monday.com admin that is looking at this topic? It would be nice to at least have an explanation on if and when this can be a thing. Or at least an explanation of why it isn’t a thing.

posts.author: davidboag (David Alexander Boag)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m confused as to how I’m supposed to even use Monday.com without the ability to quickly add items/tasks.

posts.author: sfk-funzt (Stephan Koglin-Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml: I just discovered the shortcuts by accident and was immediately disappointed that there is no “new item” shortcut available. Without it is pretty unsatisfying UX.

posts.author: Sam_H (SammH)
posts.cookedHtml:
How do we get a Monday admin to look further at this thread? I’m very confused how this still doesn’t exist natively within the app. It is very limiting to not have this functionality. Furthermore, I found the other day that this function DOES EXIST - but just not within Monday.

If you export an icloud photo to send it to Monday (while on an iphone), it will give you the options to select which board, specific item, and include any note you like in the update. See below screenshot.
IMG_4139.PNG|231x500

Seems like it should be easy to transport to within Monday itself.

Also, I really need this feature integrated into my gmail as well. This is not the same request as the functionality that the current integrations you setup in Monday for gmail offers, as those require you to pre-set which triggers would create a new item and where in Monday. This would be from within Gmail itself and more freeform. Todoist had a great integration button that lived at the top by the folder button (prior to the sidebar days) where you could just click and it would ask you where in todoist to save it to, as well as I believe if you wanted to set a date.

posts.author: brunomelo (brunomeloc)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this?

posts.author: neilthayer (Neil)
posts.cookedHtml: Dare I repeat the common response here, this is billy basic and would be the killer shortcut. Come on Monday, Clickup and other apps like Trello really sing because you can work quickly from the keyboard.

posts.author: neilthayer (Neil)
posts.cookedHtml:
…and I have found a way in which (once you click to add your first item) you can add subsequent entries.

Instead of using enter, click shift + enter and another line item appears.

posts.author: TraceJackson (Trace Jackson)
posts.cookedHtml: I have to chime in my agreeance for this feature! I’m constantly needing to add new items to all kinds of boards. Especially pre-existing boards where I want to add an item to the top of the list instead of having to scroll down a long list of items in a group just to get to the bottom and use my mouse to click Add Item. Extremely inefficient without a shortcut key.

posts.author: DecisiveDesign (Jason Oeltjen)
posts.cookedHtml: Having to navigate to a Workspace > Group to add an item is rediculously cumbersome from a project manager perspective. I should be able to add an item from the left bar or a keyboard shortcut and tell it where to be placed. This wastes a lot of my time.

posts.author: Sam_H (SammH)
posts.cookedHtml: @mondaydotcom good golly please get this into the desktop feature. It is super seamless from the iphone app (there are actually a lot of great tools in there that y’all don’t have in desktop). It’s even a widget you can have on your iphone home screen. Help us understand how this very basic functionality isn’t available on desktop yet?!

posts.author: justreed (Justin Reed)
posts.cookedHtml:
SOLUTION!

Here is a downloadable Siri Shortcut that runs directly from your phone or watch by simply saying “Create Monday Item” (you can change this to whatever you like). The setup steps will ask you for your monday.com api key and the board id you want to add items to. It’s free and your data is only sent to Monday.com (no 3rd party integrations).

https://www.justreed.com/siri-shortcut-to-create-task-in-monday-com-board-using-api/

Pro tip #1: duplicate this shortcut with different name to “create item” so you can use siri to directly add items to multiple boards.

Pro tip #2: If you set your board automation to Notify on New Item, then you will get a card pushed your phone that will enable you to add details in the newly created item with a single tap… no more messing around with searching and filtering

posts.author: mateusdso (Mateus)
posts.cookedHtml: This is HONESTLY shocking…

posts.author: sfk-funzt (Stephan Koglin-Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t want to disappoint you, but this is not a solution, it’s an ad.

posts.author: tfVu8 (Your full name)
posts.cookedHtml: This is absolutely embarrassing for Monday and shocking to me. If you needed proof that Monday.com is not fit for power users, this is clear evidence, in plain sight, extremely obvious. You expect users to click through everything? Really? Nonsense software. Get your act together Monday.

posts.author: bilzlebub (Greg Bilsland)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 - this is probably my biggest earflick with Monday right now. Having to switch to mouse every time I need create a new item is costing me and my team time every day. This shouldn’t be a big lift - please prioritize QoL upgrades like this over new shiny features.

posts.author: Sam_H (SammH)
posts.cookedHtml: They did just update to allow for easy addition of new items from the My Work page and it’s pretty decent. However, subitems are still totally lacking from the My Work page to where you can’t see the parent item without hover, nor can you can you add subitems from there.

posts.author: nikandfor (Nikifor)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree. There are shortcuts for much rarely used tasks, but not for such a basic one.

posts.author: nfietzer (Nate)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed! I was so shocked to see this wasn’t a thing. Please make it happen! It’s been on the doc for 1.5 years here and seems like it would be a relatively easy thing to do.
